subject: The farms behind your next meal
preheader: Rachel's Farm, Bundarra Berkshires, and the people who grow what you eat.
body:
One of the things that makes FarmThru different is that every product traces back to a real farm, run by a real person. Not a supplier code. A name.

Here are two of our partners.

Rachel's Farm, Kempsey NSW. Rachel Ward is a co-founder of FarmThru and a regenerative agriculture advocate. Her cattle are 100% grass-fed, raised on pasture without hormones or feedlots. After the 2020 bushfires nearly destroyed her property, she rebuilt with regenerative practices. Her beef is some of the best in the country.

Bundarra Berkshires, Bundarra NSW. Heritage Berkshire pigs, raised outdoors on open paddocks. Berkshire is one of the oldest pig breeds in the world, known for rich marbling and deep flavour. You won't find this at the supermarket.

These farmers aren't anonymous suppliers. They're partners. FarmThru's hub-and-collect model means their food goes from paddock to pick-up in 2-3 days. No warehouses. No wholesalers. Just a shorter, more honest supply chain.

We work with 50+ farms like these across NSW. Each one is regenerative. Each one is named. And each one earns a fairer price for their work.

Have a look at what's available this week.

FarmThru
creative_brief: Farm spotlight email. Hero images of Rachel's Farm and Bundarra Berkshires (landscape shots with farmers visible). Product photos below each farm story. Single CTA to shop. Educational but appetising. Food first, story second.
